theme: Was Christmas Better in the Past?

sentences.english: At this time of year, we might look back at the Christmases from the past, from our childhood, and feel like they were happier or more magical than Christmas feels today.
sentences.korean: 이맘때면 어린 시절의 크리스마스를 돌아보며 오늘날보다 더 행복하고 마법 같았다고 느낄 수 있어요.

sentences.english: Thinking about the past can make us feel happy and sad at the same time.
sentences.korean: 과거를 생각하면 행복하면서도 동시에 슬플 수 있어요.

sentences.english: It's a strange feeling we call nostalgia.
Nostalgic memories are often going to be rose-tinted and made more positive.
sentences.korean: 향수에 젖은 기억은 종종 장밋빛으로 물들어 더 긍정적이에요.

sentences.english: The function of memory is not about accuracy.
sentences.korean: 기억의 기능은 정확성에 관한 게 아니에요.

quiz.question: What does '(구어) 정말 ~하고 싶은, 간절히 원하는' mean in English?

theme: Was Christmas Better in the Past?

sentences.english: It doesn't matter if some of the details are wrong.
Catherine says nostalgic memories are rose-tinted — seeing only the positive parts of something, and not the bad parts.
sentences.korean: 캐서린은 향수에 젖은 기억이 장밋빛이라고 해요 — 긍정적인 부분만 보고 나쁜 부분은 안 보는 거죠.

sentences.english: What has become a traditional food to eat in Japan at Christmas time?
sentences.korean: 일본에서 크리스마스에 전통 음식이 된 것은?

quiz.question: What does '(구어) 정말 ~하고 싶은, 간절히 원하는' mean in English?
